meta.summary: Repo presents well overall — clear purpose, good AI front-door, and a solid demo setup — but the README project structure block is stale and carries wrong stage names and numbers that could mislead a new reader.

_guide.human_comment_or_question: optional comment; question text when human_command is ask

findings.description: Project structure block in README lists wrong filenames and stage labels. It shows 'S5_ai_analyzer.py — Stage 5 — AI analysis (planned)' and 'S5_renderer.py — Stage 6 — Report rendering (planned)', but the real analyzer file is S4_ai_analyzer.py (complete, not planned), and there is no Stage 6.
findings.recommendation: Update the structure block to use the actual filenames (S4_ai_analyzer.py, S5_renderer.py) and correct the stage labels to match the authoritative stage map in ai_context.md.
findings.how_it_stands: Removed the Project Structure code block from README.md. Coverage is preserved: the Pipeline Stages section links to ai_context.md for the authoritative stage map, and the Status table shows all stage names.

findings.description: Project structure block in README lists a 'tests/' directory, but the actual directory is 'pipeline_root/testing/'.
findings.recommendation: Replace 'tests/' with 'pipeline_root/testing/' in the structure block.
findings.how_it_stands: Updated README.md: 'tests/' replaced with 'testing/' in the project structure block (block since removed by PR-001).

findings.description: .pytest_cache/ is present at the repo root but is not listed in .gitignore.

PROJECT_PLAN.md uses ALL_CAPS while all other markdown files use Title Case (README.md) or lowercase (ai_context.md). Inconsistent, and ALL_CAPS signals something system-level rather than a planning doc.
findings.recommendation: Rename to project_plan.md or Project_Plan.md to match the prevailing convention. If it's intended as a read-only artifact, lowercase is idiomatic.

findings.description: The stage map in ai_context.md lists only JSON artifacts. README references a pipeline_root/output/ directory for HTML reports (the renderer output), but this location is not mentioned in the stage map or working rules.
findings.recommendation: Add a note to the stage map for the planned S5 renderer that its output lands in pipeline_root/output/, so the two docs agree.
findings.how_it_stands: Updated S5 renderer row in ai_context.md stage map to include the HTML output path pattern.

findings.description: reviews/*.json is gitignored, meaning all review output files (including the two that already exist locally) are not tracked. This looks intentional, but it means review history is local-only and lost on a fresh clone.
findings.recommendation: Remove 'reviews/*.json' from .gitignore and commit existing review files.
findings.how_it_stands: Removed 'reviews/*.json' from .gitignore. Review files will now be tracked.

findings.description: Requirements section says 'An AI API key, for example Anthropic’s'. The pipeline is hardwired to Claude via the anthropic SDK — it is not AI-agnostic. The phrasing implies portability that does not exist.
findings.recommendation: Replace with 'An Anthropic API key (required — pipeline runs on Claude)' or similar.
findings.how_it_stands: Updated README.md Requirements section to 'An Anthropic API key (required — pipeline runs on Claude)'.
